_comment: World music scales library for BPscript.
Temperament-based scales use 'degrees' as step positions within the grid.
Ratio-based scales use 'ratios' with exact fractions or cent strings.
Scales with different ascending/descending use 'ascending' and 'descending'.
Arabic maqams composed from jins use 'compose' and 'junction'.

bilaval.description: Bilaval thaat — equivalent to Western major scale

kafi.description: Kafi thaat — komal Ga, komal Ni

asavari.description: Asavari thaat — komal Ga, Dha, Ni

bhairavi.description: Bhairavi thaat — all komal (Re, Ga, Dha, Ni)

marva.description: Marva thaat — komal Re, tivra Ma

purvi.description: Purvi thaat — komal Re, tivra Ma, komal Dha

todi.description: Todi thaat — komal Re, Ga, tivra Ma, komal Dha

todi_carnatic.description: Shubhapantuvarali melakarta (72 #45) — equivalent to Todi thaat

malkauns.description: Raga Malkauns — audava (pentatonic), deep night raga

bageshri.description: Raga Bageshri — different notes in ascent and descent

darbari_kanada.description: Raga Darbari Kanada — andolit komal Ga, majestic night raga

desh.description: Raga Desh — komal Ni in descent, romantic evening raga

bihag.description: Raga Bihag — both Ma used, late night raga

maqam_sikah.description: Maqam Sikah — starts on sikah (E half-flat)

maqam_ajam.description: Maqam Ajam — equivalent to Western major

maqam_kurd.description: Maqam Kurd — starts with jins Kurd

maqam_suznak.description: Maqam Suznak — Rast lower + Hijaz upper

maqam_nawa_athar.description: Maqam Nawa Athar — double augmented second

maqam_athar_kurd.description: Maqam Athar Kurd — Kurd with augmented second

maqam_hijaz_kar.description: Maqam Hijaz Kar — double harmonic major

maqam_nikriz.description: Maqam Nikriz — Nikriz tetrachord + Rast upper

maqam_husayni.description: Maqam Husayni — Bayati variant with Husayni emphasis

maqam_suzidil.description: Maqam Suzidil — Ajam lower + Hijaz Kar upper

maqam_farahfaza.description: Maqam Farahfaza — Nahawand with Sikah flavor

maqam_shawq_afza.description: Maqam Shawq Afza — Sikah-based, emotional character

jins_nikriz.description: Jins Nikriz — 4-note tetrachord with augmented second

jins_athar_kurd.description: Jins Athar Kurd — Kurd variant with raised 3rd

jins_saba_zamzam.description: Jins Saba Zamzam — Saba variant with flat 2nd and 4th

jins_mustaar.description: Jins Mustaar — rare 3-note jins, narrow intervals

makam_ussak.description: Makam Ussak — one of the most common Turkish makams

makam_huseyni.description: Makam Huseyni — similar to Ussak with different upper tetrachord

makam_hicaz.description: Makam Hicaz — augmented second in lower tetrachord

makam_nihavend.description: Makam Nihavend — Turkish minor, similar to harmonic minor

makam_kurdi.description: Makam Kurdi — starts with minor second

makam_segah.description: Makam Segah — starts on segah pitch, meditative character

makam_huzzam.description: Makam Huzzam — Segah variant with diminished fifth

makam_saba.description: Makam Saba — distinctive Turkish makam with narrow intervals

makam_buselik.description: Makam Buselik — Turkish natural minor equivalent

makam_sultaniyegah.description: Makam Sultaniyegah — Rast transposed, majestic character

gong.description: Gong mode — 1st mode of Chinese pentatonic, Do position

shang.description: Shang mode — 2nd mode of Chinese pentatonic, Re position

jue.description: Jue mode — 3rd mode of Chinese pentatonic, Mi position

zhi.description: Zhi mode — 4th mode of Chinese pentatonic, Sol position

yu.description: Yu mode — 5th mode of Chinese pentatonic, La position

yayue.description: Yayue — Chinese ceremonial court music heptatonic scale

qingyue.description: Qingyue — Chinese folk heptatonic scale with minor 7th

hirajoshi.description: Hirajoshi — Japanese pentatonic scale, melancholic character

in_sen.description: In-sen — Japanese pentatonic, used in shakuhachi music

yo.description: Yo — Japanese pentatonic, bright folk scale

iwato.description: Iwato — Japanese pentatonic, dark meditative scale

miyako_bushi.description: Miyako-bushi — Japanese urban pentatonic, used in koto and shamisen

pyeong_jo.description: Pyeong-jo — Korean pentatonic mode, peaceful character

gye_myeon_jo.description: Gye-myeon-jo — Korean pentatonic mode, sorrowful character

dorian_ancient.description: Ancient Greek Dorian — descending E to E on white keys

phrygian_ancient.description: Ancient Greek Phrygian — descending D to D on white keys

lydian_ancient.description: Ancient Greek Lydian — descending C to C on white keys

mixolydian_ancient.description: Ancient Greek Mixolydian — descending B to B on white keys

chromatic_genus.description: Ancient Greek Chromatic genus tetrachord — narrow semitones + minor third

enharmonic_genus.description: Ancient Greek Enharmonic genus tetrachord — quarter-tones + major third

ionian.description: Medieval Ionian mode — C to C, equivalent to major scale

dorian.description: Medieval Dorian mode — D to D, minor with raised 6th

phrygian.description: Medieval Phrygian mode — E to E, minor with flat 2nd

lydian.description: Medieval Lydian mode — F to F, major with raised 4th

mixolydian.description: Medieval Mixolydian mode — G to G, major with flat 7th

aeolian.description: Medieval Aeolian mode — A to A, natural minor

locrian.description: Medieval Locrian mode — B to B, diminished mode

byzantine_protos.description: Byzantine Protos — 1st mode of Byzantine Octoechos

byzantine_devteros.description: Byzantine Devteros — 2nd mode of Byzantine Octoechos

tizita_major.description: Tizita major — Ethiopian pentatonic, nostalgic mood

tizita_minor.description: Tizita minor — Ethiopian pentatonic, melancholic variant

bati_major.description: Bati major — Ethiopian pentatonic, bright and festive

bati_minor.description: Bati minor — Ethiopian pentatonic, darker Bati variant

ambassel.description: Ambassel — Ethiopian pentatonic, spiritual and contemplative

anchihoye.description: Anchihoye — Ethiopian tetratonic, simplest Ethiopian mode

pelog_lima.description: Pelog lima — 5-note Javanese pelog, empirical tuning

thai_7tet.description: Thai 7-TET — 7 equal divisions of the octave

thai_pentatonic.description: Thai pentatonic — 5 of 7 equal steps, traditional Thai selection

blues.description: Blues scale — hexatonic with blue notes

whole_tone.description: Whole tone scale — 6 equal whole steps

harmonic_minor.description: Harmonic minor — natural minor with raised 7th

melodic_minor.description: Melodic minor — different ascending and descending forms

hungarian_minor.description: Hungarian minor — double harmonic minor, gypsy scale

thaat_bhairav.description: Thaat Bhairav — komal re, shuddh ga, komal dha, shuddh ni. Double Harmonic Major. Morning raga.

_comment_handpan: Handpan scales — common tunings for hang/handpan instruments. 12-TET semitone-based ratios.

handpan_kurd.description: Kurd — Aeolian / Natural Minor. The most popular handpan scale worldwide.
handpan_kurd.layout: D3 A3 Bb3 C4 D4 E4 F4 G4 A4

handpan_integral.description: Integral — Aeolian without 4th. Created by PanArt (original Hang). Open, spacious.
handpan_integral.layout: D3 A3 Bb3 C4 D4 F4 A4 C5

handpan_celtic.description: Celtic / Amara — Dorian mode. Brighter minor with raised 6th. Folk/Celtic character.
handpan_celtic.layout: D3 A3 C4 D4 E4 F4 G4 A4 B4

handpan_pygmy.description: Pygmy — Minor pentatonic + b6. African-inspired, warm and forgiving.
handpan_pygmy.layout: D3 A3 Bb3 C4 D4 F4 G4 A4 C5

handpan_equinox.description: Equinox — Phrygian mode. Dark, Spanish/Middle-Eastern flavor. Pantheon Steel.
handpan_equinox.layout: D3 A3 Bb3 C4 D4 Eb4 F4 G4 A4

handpan_hijaz.description: Hijaz — Phrygian Dominant (5th mode of Harmonic Minor). Arabic/Spanish feel.
handpan_hijaz.layout: D3 A3 Bb3 C#4 D4 E4 F4 G4 A4

handpan_hijaz_kar.description: Hijaz Kar — Double Harmonic Major / Byzantine / Bhairav. Two Hijaz tetrachords.
handpan_hijaz_kar.layout: D3 A3 Bb3 C#4 D4 E4 F4 G#4 A4

handpan_golden_gate.description: Golden Gate — Harmonic Minor. Classical sound, augmented second between b6 and 7.
handpan_golden_gate.layout: D3 A3 Bb3 C4 D4 E4 F4 G#4 A4

handpan_romanian_hijaz.description: Romanian Hijaz — Hungarian/Double Harmonic Minor. Two augmented seconds, intense Eastern European feel.
handpan_romanian_hijaz.layout: D3 A3 Bb3 C#4 D4 Eb4 F#4 G4 A4

handpan_akebono.description: Akebono — Japanese pentatonic (In scale variant). Contemplative, zen.
handpan_akebono.layout: D3 A3 Bb3 D4 E4 F4 A4 Bb4 D5

handpan_sabye.description: Sabye — PanArt creation. Mysterious, African-inspired hexatonic.
handpan_sabye.layout: D3 A3 Bb3 D4 E4 F4 G4 A4 D5

handpan_mystic.description: Mystic — Phrygian without 7th. Dark, introspective, spacious.
handpan_mystic.layout: D3 A3 Bb3 C4 D4 Eb4 G4 A4 Bb4

handpan_la_sirena.description: La Sirena — Phrygian Dominant. Spanish/Arabic dramatic character.
handpan_la_sirena.layout: D3 A3 Bb3 D4 E4 F4 A4 Bb4 C#5

handpan_oxalis.description: Oxalis — Dorian without 6th. Open, airy minor. Ayasa creation.
handpan_oxalis.layout: D3 A3 C4 D4 E4 F4 A4 C5 D5

handpan_jibuk.description: Jibuk — Mixolydian. Major-sounding with flatted 7th. Bright, festive.
handpan_jibuk.layout: D3 A3 C4 D4 E4 F#4 G4 A4 B4

handpan_annaziska.description: Annaziska — Minor Pentatonic. Maximum consonance, impossible to play wrong.
handpan_annaziska.layout: D3 A3 C4 D4 F4 G4 A4 C5 D5

handpan_ashta_taki.description: Ashta Taki — Major without 6th. Bright, uplifting, rare major handpan.
handpan_ashta_taki.layout: D3 A3 C4 D4 E4 F4 G4 B4 C5

flamenco_phrygian.description: Flamenco mode / Phrygian Dominant — the defining sound of flamenco. Hijaz maqam equivalent.

flamenco_por_medio.description: Flamenco por medio — Phrygian mode on A (guitar standard). Dark, intense.

flamenco_por_arriba.description: Flamenco por arriba — Phrygian mode on E (guitar standard). Classic flamenco position.

flamenco_double_harmonic.description: Escala andaluza / Double Harmonic Major — Hijaz Kar / Bhairav equivalent in flamenco context.

flamenco_minor.description: Flamenco minor — Harmonic minor with Andalusian cadence (iv-III-II-I).

_comment_contemporary: Contemporary and experimental scales — Messiaen, Boulez, spectral, serial.

messiaen_mode1.description: Messiaen mode 1 — Whole tone scale (6 equal divisions). Debussy, Messiaen.

messiaen_mode2.description: Messiaen mode 2 — Octatonic / Diminished (half-whole). Messiaen, Bartók, Stravinsky.

messiaen_mode3.description: Messiaen mode 3 — 9 notes, period = major third (400c). Three transpositions.

messiaen_mode4.description: Messiaen mode 4 — 8 notes, period = tritone (600c). Rare in practice.

messiaen_mode6.description: Messiaen mode 6 — 8 notes, period = tritone (600c). Augmented fourths.

messiaen_mode7.description: Messiaen mode 7 — 10 notes, period = tritone (600c). Most dense of the modes.

chromatic_12.description: Chromatic aggregate — all 12 pitch classes. Basis of serial/12-tone technique (Schoenberg, Webern, Boulez).

spectral_harmonic_8.description: Spectral scale (harmonics 8-16) — Grisey, Murail. Natural harmonic series from 8th partial.
spectral_harmonic_8._note: Ratios are partials 8-15 normalized to 8. The 11th and 13th harmonics give the characteristic non-12-TET intervals.

spectral_harmonic_16.description: Spectral scale (harmonics 1-16) — full harmonic series. Grisey Partiels, Haas.
spectral_harmonic_16._note: Selected partials from the series: 8,9,10,11,12,13,14,15 = odd harmonics emphasized.

quarter_tone_chromatic.description: Quarter-tone chromatic — 24 equal divisions. Haba, Wyschnegradsky, Boulez (Marteau sans maître).

bartok_acoustic.description: Bartók scale / Acoustic scale / Lydian Dominant — Overtone scale (Bartók, Debussy).
bartok_acoustic._note: Lydian b7. Natural harmonic approximation: partials 8-14. Also called "overtone scale".

scriabin_mystic_chord.description: Scriabin Mystic Chord / Prometheus scale — C F# Bb E A D as scale. Scriabin late works.
scriabin_mystic_chord._note: Derived from the "Prometheus chord" (quartal harmony based on tritone). Close to Messiaen mode 1 but with different emphasis.

tritone_scale.description: Tritone scale — Two augmented triads a semitone apart. Jazz/contemporary.

slonimsky_1.description: Slonimsky scale 1 — Symmetric division of octave in minor thirds + chromatic fill. Used by Coltrane.
slonimsky_1._note: From Nicolas Slonimsky "Thesaurus of Scales". Coltrane studied this extensively.

harry_partch_43.description: Harry Partch 43-tone scale — 11-limit just intonation. Microtonal pioneer.
harry_partch_43._note: 11-limit just intonation. Partch designed custom instruments for this tuning.

wendy_carlos_alpha.description: Wendy Carlos Alpha — 15.385 steps per octave (78c per step). Non-octave scale.
wendy_carlos_alpha._note: The octave (1200c) is NOT a step in this scale. Step size chosen for good approximation of 3/2 (9 steps = 702c).

wendy_carlos_beta.description: Wendy Carlos Beta — 18.809 steps per octave (63.8c per step). Non-octave scale.
wendy_carlos_beta._note: Optimized for good 3/2 (11 steps ≈ 701.6c) and 5/4 (6 steps ≈ 382.7c).

_comment_jazz: Jazz scales — bebop, modal jazz, post-bop, fusion.

bebop_dominant.description: Bebop Dominant — Mixolydian + passing natural 7th. The quintessential bebop scale (Charlie Parker, Dizzy Gillespie).
bebop_dominant._note: The added major 7th creates a chromatic passing tone between b7 and root, keeping chord tones on downbeats.

bebop_major.description: Bebop Major — Ionian + passing #5. Barry Harris method.
bebop_major._note: Added #5/b6 between 5th and 6th. Keeps 1-3-5-7 on downbeats in eighth note lines.

bebop_dorian.description: Bebop Dorian — Dorian + passing major 3rd. Minor bebop scale.
bebop_dorian._note: Chromatic passing tone between b3 and 4. Used over minor 7th chords.

bebop_melodic_minor.description: Bebop Melodic Minor — Melodic minor ascending + passing b6. David Baker.

altered.description: Altered scale / Super Locrian — 7th mode of melodic minor. Essential for V7alt chords (Coltrane, Shorter, Henderson).
altered._note: All tensions altered: b9 #9 b5 #5. The defining sound of post-bop.

lydian_augmented.description: Lydian Augmented — 3rd mode of melodic minor. #4 + #5. George Russell Lydian Chromatic Concept.

lydian_dominant.description: Lydian Dominant / Lydian b7 — 4th mode of melodic minor. Dominant sound with #4. (= Bartók acoustic scale).
lydian_dominant._note: Same as bartok_acoustic. Also called "overtone scale". Characteristic of Coltrane ballads and Herbie Hancock.

locrian_natural2.description: Locrian #2 / Half-Diminished — 6th mode of melodic minor. Used on minor7b5 chords.

phrygian_dominant.description: Phrygian Dominant — 5th mode of harmonic minor. Hijaz. Used on V7b9 in minor keys.

pentatonic_major.description: Major Pentatonic — 1 2 3 5 6. Foundation of blues, rock, jazz melody.

pentatonic_minor.description: Minor Pentatonic — 1 b3 4 5 b7. The most universal scale in popular music.

coltrane_pentatonic.description: Coltrane Pentatonic — 1 2 3 5 b7. Dominant pentatonic used by Coltrane on V7 chords.
coltrane_pentatonic._note: Also called "dominant pentatonic" or "Mixolydian pentatonic". Core of Coltrane Changes sound.

kumoi_jazz.description: Kumoi (jazz usage) — 1 2 b3 5 6. Japanese-influenced pentatonic popular in jazz (McCoy Tyner).

in_sen_jazz.description: In-Sen — 1 b2 4 5 b7. Japanese scale used in jazz (John McLaughlin, Joe Henderson).

augmented_scale.description: Augmented scale — Symmetric scale alternating m3 and H. Coltrane, Thelonious Monk.
augmented_scale._note: Two augmented triads a semitone apart. Same as Messiaen mode 3 subset. Only 4 transpositions.

tritone_dominant.description: Tritone scale (dominant) — Two major triads a tritone apart. Mark Levine, modern jazz.
tritone_dominant._note: C E G + F# A# C# = all notes of two V7 chords a tritone apart. Foundation of tritone substitution.
